diagrams.title: IoT Architecture Layers
diagrams.description: Shows the four-layer IoT architecture with security considerations at each layer
elements: Perception Layer (Sensors, Actuators) - Physical security, Device authentication
Network Layer (Wi-Fi, Zigbee, BLE) - Encryption, Protocol security
Processing Layer (Edge Computing, Cloud) - Data security, Access control
Application Layer (User Interfaces, APIs) - Authentication, Data privacy
diagrams.purpose: Understanding where security measures must be implemented across IoT systems

diagrams.title: WPA2 4-Way Handshake with KRACK Attack
diagrams.description: Illustrates the WPA2 handshake process and where KRACK attack intervenes
elements: Step 1: AP sends ANonce to device
Step 4: Device acknowledges - KRACK intercepts here by replaying message
Key reinstallation allows attacker to reset nonce values
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how KRACK exploits the handshake mechanism to decrypt traffic

diagrams.title: IoT Device Lifecycle Security
diagrams.description: Shows security considerations at each stage of IoT device lifecycle
elements: Operation - Firmware updates, monitoring, access control
Decommission - Data wiping, factory reset, secure disposal
diagrams.purpose: Understanding security requirements throughout the entire device lifetime
